data.title: BJ's Restaurants' promos lure in diners, drives comp sales higher
data.description:
With the assistance of promotional meal deals and holiday offerings, increased traffic (+5.5%) to BJ’s Restaurant (NASDAQ:BJRI) drove sales 6.4% higher during the fourth quarter and to an increase in (non-GAAP) profitability, beating Wall Street’s expectations and launching shares 12% higher in Friday’s premarket trading.

The stock is expected to open at its highest level in over three years.

“Our fourth quarter results reflect significant progress in our efforts to drive sales and improve margins,” said CEO Brad Richmond, noting the 5.5% gain in comparable restaurant sales and 100 basis point improvement in operating profit margin.

As increased expenses outstripped the 6.4% gain in sales, net income swung to a loss in Q4 of $5.27M, or $0.23 per share. However, taking out certain items, the restaurant chain earned a profit of $0.47 per share, 2 cents better than last year and 13 cents better than expected. Adjusted EBITDA increased 9.6% to $33.1M.

For FY25, BJ’s Restaurants (NASDAQ:BJRI) expects comparable restaurant sales to grow by 2% to 3%, leading to restaurant level operating profit between $205M to $215M and adjusted EBITDA of $127M to $137M.

data.title: U.S. attorneys general forum urges FDA to act against counterfeit weight loss drugs
data.description:
A bipartisan group of U.S. state and territory attorneys general is pressing the FDA to take prompt action against those threatening public health by marketing counterfeit forms of branded weight loss medications developed by Novo Nordisk (NVO) and Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY).

Their concerns, communicated to Sara Brenner, the acting commissioner of the FDA on Thursday, come at a time when the two leading obesity drugmakers struggle to maintain an adequate supply of their hugely popular GLP-1 meds.

“The FDA must work with federal partners like the Department of Homeland Security to intercept counterfeit GLP-1 drugs before they reach unsuspecting consumers,” the group representing 38 state and territory attorneys general wrote.

Their letter also highlighted online retailers selling the active ingredients of weight loss drugs without requiring prescriptions and compound pharmacies "illegally participating" in the obesity drug market.

“Much like with counterfeit versions, these active ingredients come from unregulated, undisclosed sources in countries like China and India and pose risks of contamination and inclusion of foreign substances.”

“With consumers looking to compounding pharmacies to meet the high demand, insanitary conditions at these facilities could lead to serious public health issues.”

The group led by attorneys general in South Carolina, Colorado, Illinois, and Tennessee noted that the FDA has expertise and resources to ensure the safety of GLP-1 supplies available in the U.S. “We urge the FDA to exercise its statutory authority through investigations, inspections, and enforcement actions to safeguard consumers.”

data.title: National Bank of Canada scores Buy rating in new BofA coverage
data.description:
Bank of America Securities intiated coverage of National Bank of Canada (OTCPK:NTIOF) with a Buy rating, as analyst Ebrahim H. Poonawala sees a significant opportunity for the bank to boost its business beyond its home turf of Quebec.

"As the smallest Canadian bank by assets, we view National as a rare secular growth story in a saturated banking market with significant runway to gain share outside its home market of Quebec," the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

The company has hired bankers with a strong focus on commercial and wealth businesses outside of Quebec and purchased Canadian Western Bank, which closed on Feb. 3, he pointed out. Poonawala also expects a volatile FICC trading backdrop to provide a tailwind to National's (OTCPK:NTIOF) niche capital markets business.

BofA's Buy rating on National (OTCPK:NTIOF) contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Sell and the average Wall Street rating of Hold.

data.title: Dropbox falls after outlook miss; to keep FormSwift despite impact on revenue growth
data.description:
Shares of Dropbox (NASDAQ:DBX) fell about 7% premarket on Friday after the company's outlook came in below expectations.

For the first quarter of 2025, the company expects revenue to be between $618M and $621M (midpoint at $619.5M), compared to a consensus estimate of $625.87M. The company expects a currency headwind of about $3M.

On a constant currency basis, Dropbox expects revenue to be in the range of $621M to $624M (midpoint at $622.5M).

For the full-year 2025, Dropbox anticipates revenue to be between $2.465B and $2.480B (midpoint at $2.473B), versus consensus of $2.53B. The company expects a currency headwind of around $18M.

The updated outlook comes on the back of certain business/macro updates provided by Dropbox's CFO Tim Regan on the company's fourth quarter earnings call on Thursday since they shared early thinking on 2025 expectations during the November earnings call.

Regan said the company will continue its "ownership of FormSwift, while concurrently significantly reducing our headcount and eliminating our marketing investment in that business."

Regan expects that "this approach will thereby serve as a headwind to revenue growth over the next few years, though it will also serve as a tailwind to free cash flow."

Secondly, Regan said that the U.S. dollar meaningfully strengthened since the company shared its early thinking on 2025, impacting both revenue and free cash flow expectations for 2025.

In addition, the CFO noted that, considering the company's recent capital raise, Dropbox will start to guide to unlevered free cash flow, which they define as free cash flow excluding the impact of interest payments associated with term loans net of their associated tax benefits.

Q4: metrics: For the fourth quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024 Dropbox's revenue rose 1.4% year-over-year to $643.6M, while non-GAAP EPS jumped 46% year-over-year to $0.73. Both top and bottom line beat estimates.

data.title: Lumen Technologies upgraded at Wells Fargo on PCF sales, quantum fiber potential
data.description:
Lumen Technologies (NYSE:LUMN) was upgraded at Wells Fargo on Friday as the investment firm said there are several positives for the telecom company, including private connectivity fabric-related sales and potential for its quantum fiber unit.

Shares rose 2% in premarket trading.

“By our est, the $8.5B of [private connectivity fabric] sales announced thus far have benefited 2024/2025 FCF by nearly $2.5B,” Wells Fargo analysts wrote in a note to clients. “And while we suspect FCF will turn negative in 2026 (higher capex, higher cash taxes), it seems likely LUMN will ink additional dark fiber sales throughout 2025 that will provide an additional FCF boost. Regardless, with [more than] $2B of cash available by YE'25 and EBITDA improving by 2026, LUMN's liquidity position has been substantially boosted. Quantum Fiber sale could provide upside catalyst.”

The analysts raised their rating on Lumen Technologies to Equal-Weight from Underweight and maintained their $5 price target.

If Lumen were to carve out and sell its fiber business, it could value the unit at roughly $6B and be worth at least $800M to Lumen's free cash flow by 2026. This would in turn bring down its net leverage by more than 1 time by 2026.

If Lumen sells the entire Mass Markets business, it could be worth $8B and boost free cash flow by $200M, while deleveraging it by roughly 0.5 times by 2026.

data.title: Arbor Realty Trust turns in mixed Q4, with net interest income beat, small EPS miss
data.description:
Arbor Realty Trust (NYSE:ABR) turned in a mixed quarter as Q4 earnings just missed the average analyst estimate and net interest income beat. The mortgage REIT's fee-based servicing portfolio and its agency loan originations grew during the quarter, while its structured business loan and investment portfolio decreased.

Q4 distributable EPS of $0.40, missing the average analyst estimate of $0.41, fell from $0.43 in Q3 and $0.51 in Q4 2023.

Net interest income of $82.9M, vs.the Visible Alpha consensus of $80.4M, decreased from $88.8M in the prior quarter and $103.6M in the year-ago quarter.

Arbor Realty (NYSE:ABR) stock rose 0.3% in Friday premarket trading.

Total other revenue, consisting of gains on sales, mortgage servicing rights, net servicing revenue, among other items, climbed to $68.8M from $67.8M in Q3 and dropped from $85.3M in Q4 2023.

The company’s fee-based servicing portfolio totaled $33.5B at Dec. 31, 2024, compared with $33.0B at Sept. 30.

Agency loan originations climbed to $1.38B from $1.10B in the prior quarter. Its agency business generated revenue of $78.7M vs. $77.4M in the previous quarter.

Its structured business loan and investment portfolio’s unpaid principal balance, excluding loan loss reserves, was $11.3B at Dec. 31, with a weighted average interest rate of 6.90%, compared with $11.6B and 7.25% at Sept. 30.

Conference call at 10 AM ET.

Earlier, Arbor Realty Trust non-GAAP EPS of $0.40 misses by $0.01, revenue of $82.67M misses by $1.61M

data.title: Posco surges as Korea set to slap anti-dumping duties on Chinese steel
data.description:
Posco Holdings (NYSE:PKX) +4.2% pre-market Friday after closing 4.7% higher in the previous session, as South Korea's government moved to impose anti-dumping duties on Chinese steel products in an effort to protect the local steel industry.

The Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy said its trade commission recommended imposing as much as 38% provisional anti-dumping duties on Chinese hot-rolled thick steel plates starting next month, and will launch a full state investigation into the alleged Chinese dumping.

An anti-dumping complaint by Hyundai Steel last July against Chinese rivals prompted the South Korean commission to start a four-month preliminary probe in October.

Korean steelmakers have struggled with weak demand at home and abroad, and they say the oversupply of steel products from China has aggravated the industry downturn.

Posco (NYSE:PKX) posted a net loss in Q4, and its full year net profit fell by nearly half to 948B won while operating profit sank 38% to 2.17T won and revenue fell 5.8% to 72.69T won.

data.title: Snowflake in spotlight as TD Cowen sees 'constructive' partner checks ahead of Q4 results
data.description:
Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW) was in the spotlight on Friday as TD Cowen said recent partner checks were “constructive” ahead of the data processing and warehouse company's fourth-quarter results next week.

Shares were little changed in premarket trading.

“Our partner checks were constructive, citing growth stability in core SQL workloads, increasing traction w/ new products (especially Cortex AI Search) & unstructured data, positive market reception around Iceberg and improving GTM motions (engaging w/ more tech users),” TD Cowen analysts wrote in a note to clients. “Expecting solid 4Q out-performance & see room for FY26 numbers to move higher.”

The analysts reiterated their Buy rating on Snowflake and upped their price target to $210 from $190.

Looking to the fourth-quarter, the analysts said one partner saw its Snowflake business grow 29% in the period, above its target range of 25% to 28%. It is also targeting roughly 30% growth in fiscal 2026, with areas like larger scale cloud modernization, increased traction with Snowpark and Cortez, “AI firepower” and added flexibility. There was also an improvement in Snowflake's go-to-market initiatives, with “greater involvement” from tech buyers.

“We look for upside returning to [trailing 12-month average] levels (~3.5%) vs. high-end of guide at ~23%, and we note there was likely extra holiday/OOO seasonality this [quarter] (as was expected),” the analysts wrote. “We think Street at 22% growth for FY26 is conservative (we model 23%) and see room for numbers to be walked up.”

A consensus of analysts expect Snowflake to earn $0.18 per share on $955.52M in revenue.
